Title: The chromosome-level reference genome assembly for Panax notoginseng and insights into ginsenoside biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to dammarane-type triterpene acceptors at C20 of protopanaxadiol and protopanaxatriol, producing CK and F1, and further glycosylates Rh2 to produce F2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; roots/rhizomes are discussed as likely sites of further modification/accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Glycosylation described as the ultimate and most critical step determining saponin structure and pharmacological effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of specific ginsenosides by C20 glycosylation and additional glycosylation to F2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21(DE3); in vitro crude enzyme assay with UDP-glucose and triterpene substrates; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnUGT1 catalyzed C20 glucosylation of PPD and PPT to produce CK and F1, respectively. 2. PnUGT1 also converted Rh2 to F2, identified as a newly reported function in P. notoginseng. 3. The enzyme was one of five UGTs experimentally validated for ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Clustered with pathway genes; expression categories across tissues indicate many pathway-related UGTs are high in flowers, with some high in roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly (NCBI BioProject PRJNA658419; Genome Warehouse GWHAOSA00000000); transcriptome RNA-seq (accession not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue context: 1- to 4-year-old plants; root, stem, leaf, flower, rhizome, fibril, periderm, phloem, tubercle
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function consistent with previously reported P. ginseng UGTPg100/UGTPg101 homolog activities; also shown catalytic in Saccharomyces cerevisiae in cited prior work
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for metabolic engineering of specific ginsenosides such as CK, F1, and F2; may be stacked with upstream triterpene pathway genes and complementary UGTs to tailor ginsenoside profiles

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Extends the sugar chain on Rh2 to generate Rg3 using UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and roots/rhizomes; PnUGT2 specifically noted among UGTs highly expressed in rhizomes/roots in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Glycosylation described as the ultimate and most critical step determining saponin structure and pharmacological effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of Rh2 to Rg3 by sugar-chain extension
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21(DE3); in vitro crude enzyme assay; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnUGT2 catalyzed conversion of Rh2 to Rg3. 2. It was assigned to the blue WGCNA module correlated with pathway genes. 3. It is one of the post-modification UGTs highlighted as highly expressed in roots/rhizomes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included in blue WGCNA module; discussed as post-modification UGT highly expressed in rhizomes/roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly (PRJNA658419; GWHAOSA00000000); transcriptome RNA-seq (accession not stated); WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue context: 1- to 4-year-old plants; multiple organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function consistent with reported P. ginseng UGTPg29-related activity; also experimentally shown catalytic in Saccharomyces cerevisiae in cited work
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering Rg3 enrichment and late-stage tailoring of dammarane saponins

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds a glucosyl group at the C6 position of PPT and F1 to produce Rh1 and Rg1, respectively
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and roots/rhizomes; PnUGT3 specifically noted among UGTs highly expressed in rhizomes/roots in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Glycosylation described as the ultimate and most critical step determining saponin structure and pharmacological effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C6 glucosylation in PPT-type ginsenoside biosynthesis, producing Rh1 and Rg1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21(DE3); in vitro crude enzyme assay; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnUGT3 glucosylated PPT at C6 to form Rh1. 2. PnUGT3 glucosylated F1 at C6 to form Rg1. 3. Its activity matched previously reported P. ginseng UGTPg1/UGTPg101 functions but was newly cloned from P. notoginseng.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Discussed as post-modification UGT highly expressed in rhizomes/roots; pathway-related expression also often highest in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly (PRJNA658419; GWHAOSA00000000); transcriptome RNA-seq (accession not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue context: 1- to 4-year-old plants; multiple organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: New gene cloned for the first time in P. notoginseng; homologous functional correspondence to P. ginseng UGTPg1/UGTPg101
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering PPT-type ginsenoside products, especially Rg1 accumulation

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Extends the sugar chain on Rh2 to generate Rg3 using UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers and roots/rhizomes; PnUGT4 specifically noted among UGTs highly expressed in rhizomes/roots in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Glycosylation described as the ultimate and most critical step determining saponin structure and pharmacological effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of Rh2 to Rg3 by sugar-chain extension
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21(DE3); in vitro crude enzyme assay; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnUGT4 catalyzed conversion of Rh2 to Rg3. 2. It is one of five experimentally validated UGTs in this study. 3. It was highlighted among post-modification UGTs highly expressed in roots/rhizomes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Discussed as post-modification UGT highly expressed in rhizomes/roots; pathway-related expression also often highest in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly (PRJNA658419; GWHAOSA00000000); transcriptome RNA-seq (accession not stated)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue context: 1- to 4-year-old plants; multiple organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also experimentally shown catalytic in Saccharomyces cerevisiae in cited work
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering Rg3 production and tailoring late glycosylation patterns

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates PPD to produce Rh2 using UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Flowers; roots/rhizomes also implicated for some post-modification steps
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate/target product in late glycosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Glycosylation described as the ultimate and most critical step determining saponin structure and pharmacological effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of Rh2 from PPD, providing substrate for subsequent Rg3 synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli BL21(DE3); in vitro crude enzyme assay; UPLC/Q-TOF-MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PnUGT5 converted PPD to Rh2. 2. PnUGT5 was included in the green WGCNA module correlated with pathway genes. 3. It functions upstream of PnUGT2/PnUGT4-mediated Rg3 formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Included in green WGCNA module; pathway-related expression often enriched in flowers, with some root-associated expression classes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly (PRJNA658419; GWHAOSA00000000); transcriptome RNA-seq (accession not stated); WGCNA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developmental stage/tissue context: 1- to 4-year-old plants; multiple organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also experimentally shown catalytic in Saccharomyces cerevisiae in cited work
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering Rh2/Rg3 branch flux when combined with downstream UGTs
